claudeAnalysis.outcomePrediction: Labor holds a comfortable 52–48 two-party-preferred lead nationally, making them strong favourites for the 2028 federal election, but two state elections in 2026 will test the Albanese government's mid-term standing.
keyIssues: cost of living and grocery prices
claudeAnalysis.electionBriefing: Australia faces two significant state elections in 2026 before the next federal poll. South Australia votes on March 21 where Labor seeks a third term, and Queensland votes in October where the new LNP government will face its first re-election test. Federally, Albanese's Labor holds a healthy two-party lead of 52–48, but housing and cost-of-living pressures remain the key political battleground.
riskFactors: housing crisis eroding Labor's core vote in capital cities
Greens preference deals shifting in Coalition's favour
